meta_title: Roofing in Weston, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Expert roofing in Weston, FL. Hurricane-rated CertainTeed shingles & West Lake tile systems. Licensed, local, A+ BBB. Free estimates — call Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today!
h1: Weston's Trusted Roofing Specialists: Hurricane-Rated Systems Built for South Florida's Toughest Storms
intro:
Weston, Florida is one of Broward County's most prestigious master-planned communities — a place where manicured streetscapes, top-rated schools, and beautifully maintained homes command average values around $550,000. From the grand estate lots of Windmill Ranch Estates to the resort-style neighborhoods of Weston Hills, Savanna, The Ridges, and Emerald Estates, homeowners here invest deeply in their properties. That investment deserves a roof that performs at the highest level — one engineered to withstand South Florida's fierce weather patterns, protect structural integrity, and maintain the curb appeal these neighborhoods are known for.

Weston sits approximately 15 miles inland from the Atlantic coast, which might seem like a buffer — but make no mistake, the city sits squarely in a 140 mph wind zone, and Broward County's storm history proves that inland communities are not immune to catastrophic damage. Hurricane Andrew reshaped how the entire region thought about residential construction. Hurricanes Wilma and Irma left their marks on Weston's tree canopy and rooflines alike. With eight significant storm events since 1990, the roofing systems on Weston homes must be chosen and installed with genuine respect for what South Florida weather can deliver.

Approximately 15% of Weston's homes were built before 1994 — the watershed year in which Florida's building codes were fundamentally restructured in the aftermath of Andrew. Homes in this category are particularly vulnerable to modern storm loads and may be operating with outdated underlayments, insufficient fastening patterns, and aging shingle or tile systems that no longer meet current performance standards. But even newer homes experience natural wear: UV degradation, thermal cycling, wind-driven rain infiltration, and algae growth all shorten roof lifespans in ways that aren't always visible until significant damage has occurred. Whether your Weston home needs a full roof replacement or targeted repairs, acting proactively — rather than reactively — is the single best thing you can do to protect your family and your investment.

section_why.h2: Why Weston Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing as Their Roofing Contractor
section_why.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built its reputation across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties by delivering one thing consistently: exceptional craftsmanship backed by genuine accountability. We are not a fly-by-night operation that blows into town after a storm and disappears once the checks clear. We are a permanent fixture in South Florida's home improvement landscape — licensed, insured, A+ rated by the Better Business Bureau, and proud to have completed roofing projects on more than 500 homes throughout the region. Weston homeowners can trust that when Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is on the job, they're working with professionals who understand this community's standards.

One of the most significant differentiators that sets Premier Impact Windows & Roofing apart from the competition is our 100% in-house installation model. Every single project — from initial measurements and permit applications to final inspections — is executed by our own trained roofing crews. We never subcontract. In an industry where subs of subs of subs can erode quality at every hand-off, this distinction matters enormously. Our team brings over 60 years of combined roofing experience to each job, and that institutional knowledge is reflected in the details: proper nail patterns for Broward County's wind zone requirements, correctly installed self-adhering underlayments, precisely set tile spacing, and thorough deck inspections before any new material ever goes down.

Weston's neighborhoods each have their own architectural personalities. The barrel tile roofs of Windmill Ranch Estates require a completely different approach than the architectural shingles common in Savanna or Weston Hills. Our team is fluent in both — and in the HOA documentation requirements that govern roofing choices in many of Weston's communities. We handle permit submissions directly with the City of Weston Building Division, coordinate all required inspections, and keep homeowners informed at every stage of the process.

From financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options, to transparent project timelines and post-installation follow-up, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing treats every Weston roof as if it were our own. We call ourselves the Kings of Service not as a marketing slogan but as a daily operational standard. When you invite us onto your property, you get a team that shows up on time, cleans up completely, and stands behind its work long after the final shingle is set.

section_history.h2: Weston's Storm Legacy: Why a High-Performance Roof Is Non-Negotiable in This Community
section_history.body:
Weston's storm history is a sobering reminder that no zip code in South Florida is truly sheltered from nature's power. Hurricane Andrew in August of 1992 — before Weston's master-planned development had even reached full buildout — demonstrated with devastating clarity how catastrophically inadequate residential roofing systems could fail under extreme wind loads. Andrew's direct path across South Broward and Miami-Dade decimated tens of thousands of roofs and triggered a complete overhaul of Florida's building code system. The aftermath of Andrew is still the defining event in how this region thinks about residential construction and roofing standards.

Hurricane Wilma, which made landfall in October 2005 as a powerful Category 3 storm, tracked directly across Broward County and caused widespread roof damage throughout Weston's established neighborhoods. Many homeowners discovered that even relatively modern roofs — those built in the mid-to-late 1990s — weren't performing as expected under sustained high winds. Wilma's damage led to a new wave of roof replacements across communities like Bonaventure and the Ridges, and renewed conversations about the importance of secondary water barriers and improved underlayment systems. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, which delivered prolonged wind exposure across Broward County, toppling trees, compromising flashing systems, and revealing chronic weaknesses in aging rooflines throughout the city.

With eight named storm events affecting the Weston area since 1990, the statistical reality is straightforward: Weston homeowners will face major wind events again. The question is whether your roof will be ready when it happens. A properly installed, hurricane-rated roofing system is the primary defense your home has against water intrusion, structural compromise, and the chain reaction of losses that follows when a roof fails. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we design every Weston roofing project with that reality front of mind.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Compliance for Weston Roofing: What Every Broward County Homeowner Must Know
section_code.body:
Weston falls under the jurisdiction of Broward County's Florida Building Code requirements and, while it is not classified within the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation that applies specifically to Miami-Dade and Broward's coastal municipalities — the city still carries significant roofing code obligations. Weston is located in a 140 mph wind speed design zone, which means all roofing systems must be engineered and installed to resist the uplift, shear, and racking forces associated with sustained winds at that threshold. This is not a technicality; it is a life-safety requirement that directly governs the products, fastening schedules, and installation methods that are permissible on your home.

Under the current Florida Building Code (FBC), roofing replacements in Weston require permits issued through the City of Weston Building Division, located at Weston's Town Center administrative campus. Permit applications must include product approval documentation demonstrating that all roofing materials — shingles, tile, underlayment, fasteners, and adhesives — carry Florida Product Approvals (FPA) and are rated for the local design wind speed. Inspections are required at key installation milestones, including deck inspection, dry-in inspection, and final inspection. Skipping permits is not just illegal — it can void your homeowner's insurance, complicate future sales, and expose you to significant liability if storm damage occurs on an unpermitted roof.

For homes built before 1994, roofing replacement projects also typically require a secondary water barrier — a self-adhering modified bitumen underlayment that provides a critical layer of protection in the event that primary roofing materials are displaced during a storm. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles all permitting directly with the City of Weston Building Division and ensures that every product we install carries the appropriate Florida Product Approvals for Broward County's wind zone. We do the paperwork so you don't have to, and we make sure every project is fully inspected and code-compliant before we consider the job complete.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Architectural Shingles and West Lake Tile Systems: Premium Roofing Products Built for Weston Homes
section_products.body:
At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've carefully selected our roofing product lines based on one criterion above all others: performance under South Florida's specific conditions. That's why we install CertainTeed's Landmark Series architectural shingles and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems — two product families that represent the best combination of wind resistance, durability, aesthetics, and long-term value available to Weston homeowners today.

CertainTeed's Landmark Series is one of the most respected architectural shingle systems in North America, and it's an excellent choice for Weston homeowners who want a high-performance roof with classic curb appeal at a more accessible price point than tile. The Landmark shingles feature a dual-layered construction that delivers exceptional wind resistance, and select products within the line are rated for winds up to 130 mph with enhanced fastening patterns that can be applied to meet Broward County's 140 mph design requirements. CertainTeed shingles are algae-resistant — a critical feature in South Florida's humid subtropical climate — and carry robust manufacturer warranties that protect Weston homeowners for decades. The Landmark Series is available in a wide palette of colors and profiles that complement the architectural character of neighborhoods from Savanna to Emerald Estates.

For homeowners in communities like Windmill Ranch Estates or The Ridges, where Mediterranean and Spanish Colonial architecture is the visual standard, West Lake concrete and clay tile systems offer an unmatched combination of authenticity and strength. West Lake tile products are engineered to meet Florida's strict product approval standards and hold up exceptionally well against UV degradation, thermal cycling, and impact events. Concrete tile provides incredible mass and durability, while clay tile offers that classic warm patina associated with South Florida's finest homes. Both systems, when installed with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's in-house crews using proper mortar or foam adhesive application and hurricane clip systems, deliver roofing performance that meets and exceeds Broward County code — and looks stunning doing it.

section_cost.h2: What Does a New Roof Cost in Weston, FL? Investment, Financing, and Long-Term Value
section_cost.body:
Weston homeowners understand that their properties are significant investments — the area's average home value of approximately $550,000 reflects a community that takes pride in quality and long-term stewardship. Roofing is one of the most impactful investments you can make in that home, directly affecting structural integrity, energy efficiency, curb appeal, and resale value. Pricing for a full roof replacement in Weston varies based on the size of the home, the roofing system selected, and the complexity of the installation — but generally, homeowners in the area can expect architectural shingle replacements to range from approximately $15,000 to $30,000, while concrete or clay tile systems typically range from $25,000 to $55,000 or more for larger estate-sized homes. These are not costs to minimize with the cheapest available contractor; they are investments to maximize with the best available craftspeople.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes premium roofing accessible to every Weston homeowner through flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month, with $0 down programs available for qualified applicants. Our financing is designed to eliminate the barrier between a homeowner who knows they need a new roof and actually getting that roof done right — before the next storm season arrives. For homeowners managing significant projects or combining roofing with other improvements like impact windows, our financing team can structure solutions that fit real household budgets without compromising on product quality or installation standards.

From a return-on-investment perspective, a new hurricane-rated roof in Weston typically delivers in multiple ways: real estate appraisers consistently recognize a quality roof as a value-add at the point of sale, insurance carriers may substantially reduce annual premiums following a roof replacement with wind mitigation credits, and energy-efficient roofing materials can reduce cooling loads in Florida's relentless summer heat. When you factor in the avoided costs of water damage, interior repairs, and emergency storm response, a properly installed roof pays for itself many times over across its service life.

section_insurance.h2: Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance Premiums with a New Roof: What Weston Homeowners Need to Know
section_insurance.body:
In Broward County's insurance market, the age and condition of your roof is one of the single most powerful variables affecting your annual homeowner's insurance premium. Florida's property insurance carriers — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, Heritage Insurance, and dozens of others operating in Weston's market — use roof age, construction type, and documented wind mitigation features as core underwriting factors. A roof that is 15 or more years old may trigger dramatically higher premiums, coverage restrictions, or even non-renewal notices. Conversely, a new roof — particularly one installed with documented wind mitigation features — can unlock substantial savings.

The key financial tool in this process is the Wind Mitigation Inspection Report, commonly called a wind mit. After Premier Impact Windows & Roofing completes your roofing project, a licensed inspector can come to your home and document the specific features of your new roof: the shape (hip roofs earn the best credits), the roof deck attachment method, the secondary water barrier, and the type and rating of the roofing covering itself. This report is submitted to your insurance carrier, who then applies credits based on the documented features. Weston homeowners with qualifying roofs have historically seen annual premium reductions ranging from 10% to 40% or more — savings that can amount to thousands of dollars per year on a home valued at $550,000.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing works with our clients to ensure that every roofing installation is documented in a way that maximizes wind mitigation credit eligibility. We use fastening schedules and underlayment systems specifically designed to score well on wind mitigation inspections, and we walk homeowners through the post-installation inspection process so they know exactly what to expect. Getting a new roof from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing isn't just about protecting your home from the next storm — it's about immediately improving your financial position as a Weston homeowner.

faqs.q: How do I know if my Weston home's roof needs to be replaced versus repaired?
faqs.a: The decision depends on several factors: the age of your current roof (most shingle roofs have a 20-25 year lifespan in South Florida's climate; tile systems can last longer with proper maintenance), the extent and pattern of damage, and whether your current system meets current Florida Building Code standards. If your roof is approaching 15+ years old, shows widespread granule loss, has repeated leak points, or was installed before the 1994 post-Andrew code revisions, a full replacement is typically the more cost-effective long-term choice. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers free on-site evaluations to give Weston homeowners a clear, honest assessment.

faqs.q: What does roofing financing look like for a Weston homeowner, and how do I qualify?
faqs.a: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing options starting at $87 per month with $0 down programs available for qualified applicants. Our financing is available for both full roof replacements and significant repair projects, and approval is typically fast — often same-day. We work with multiple lending partners to find solutions that match different credit profiles, so even homeowners who've been told 'no' elsewhere may have options. During your free estimate, our team will walk you through all available financing structures so you can make a fully informed decision with no pressure.

faqs.q: What permits are required for a roof replacement in Weston, and does Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handle them?
faqs.a: Yes — Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles all permitting directly with the City of Weston Building Division on your behalf. Roofing replacements in Weston require a building permit, and all materials must carry Florida Product Approvals appropriate for Broward County's 140 mph wind zone. Required inspections include deck, dry-in, and final inspections. We prepare and submit all permit documentation, schedule inspections, and ensure every phase of the project is fully code-compliant. Homeowners never need to navigate city hall on their own.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement typically take for a Weston home?
faqs.a: For most Weston single-family homes, a full roof replacement takes between one and three days for architectural shingle systems, and two to five days for concrete or clay tile systems, depending on roof size and complexity. Permit approval through the City of Weston Building Division typically adds one to two weeks to the pre-construction timeline. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides a clear project schedule before work begins, and our in-house crews are known for efficient, focused execution — we don't leave jobs half-finished or bounce crews between multiple sites simultaneously.

faqs.q: Will a new roof actually lower my homeowner's insurance premium in Weston?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — and significantly so. After Premier Impact Windows & Roofing completes your roofing project, a wind mitigation inspector can document the specific hurricane-resistant features of your new roof. This Wind Mitigation Report is submitted to your insurance carrier and can qualify your home for substantial discounts — sometimes 10% to 40% annually — on your homeowner's insurance premium. On a high-value Weston home, those savings can be thousands of dollars per year, making the return on a new roof even more compelling than the upfront investment alone suggests.
